Describe the feature

Astropy Table lets you search through the results in a notebook. 

By default, our tables do not have this.
The call is a different one but there must be a way to hook into it and display it like that in a notebook. 

See https://docs.astropy.org/en/stable/api/astropy.table.Table.html#astropy.table.Table.show_in_notebook

It is going away, but we should still do it.

### Proposed solution

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

dictionary","user":{"login":"Prtm2110","id":139000226,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEj5og","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139000226?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110","html_url":"https://github.com/Prtm2110","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"## PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

dictionary","user":{"login":"Prtm2110","id":139000226,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEj5og","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139000226?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110","html_url":"https://github.com/Prtm2110","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"## PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

dictionary","user":{"login":"Prtm2110","id":139000226,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEj5og","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139000226?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110","html_url":"https://github.com/Prtm2110","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"## PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

Fixes #7639 ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7899/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7899/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/2526351035","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7899#issuecomment-2526351035","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7899","id":2526351035,"node_id":"IC_kwDOACEKh86WlQ67","user":{"login":"Prtm2110","id":139000226,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEj5og","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139000226?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110","html_url":"https://github.com/Prtm2110","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-08T19:50:31Z","updated_at":"2024-12-08T19:50:31Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"
> I assume astropy picked the other library for a reason?
> 
I believe maybe they didn't pick itables because "itables appears to change how dataframes are rendered Notebook-wide which I felt isn't something we're going for." said by exitflynn, although this is not a problem now because itable have `show(df)` method which only converts particular dataframes rather than all.

> What is the difference between them?

If we use itables we can directly use their show method which can display them in jupyter notbook, but in astropy they have used ipydatagrid to single tables and returned them directly, which we cannot do in sunpy as there can be multiple tables in a fido result, also this is the reason why we cannot use astropy's show_in_notebook method.

> Also is this an AI answer?
 
Neither the code nor the comments are ai (although I use grammarly), I guess it is a problem? I will stop that now.
> > Additionally, do you have any suggestions for writing tests for this integration?
> 
> Honestly, I do not. How did astropy do it?
> 
> Since astropy are dealing with these types of dependencies, I think investigating how they do that and copying their solution is the simplest path forward.

Yes, they just checked `isInstance(t.show_in_table(), Datagrid)` but again this wouldn't work here, because we are not returning Datagrid as they are.

> > 
> > 
> > I believe maybe they didn't pick itables because "itables appears to change how dataframes are rendered Notebook-wide which I felt isn't something we're going for." said by exitflynn, although this is not a problem now because itable have `show(df)` method which only converts particular dataframes rather than all.
> 
> How does it change them compared to the other library?
> 
`itable` has this feature as well
```
from itables import init_notebook_mode

init_notebook_mode(all_interactive=True)
```
This converts all dataframes into those interactive tables, this is what exitflynn meant although i guess he missed or they added `show(df)` new method later, which makes changes to particular dataframes only 
> > > What is the difference between them?
> > 
> > 
> > If we use itables we can directly use their show method which can display them in jupyter notbook, but in astropy they have used ipydatagrid to single tables and returned them directly, which we cannot do in sunpy as there can be multiple tables in a fido result, also this is the reason why we cannot use astropy's show_in_notebook method.
> 
> So how does both libraries handle having multiple tables and rendering them in a notebook?
> 

Naturally they cannot render multiple tables, but `itable` renders the tables in distinct boxes with search button (image in the comment above) which looks good.

> > > > Additionally, do you have any suggestions for writing tests for this integration?
> > > 
> > > 
> > > Honestly, I do not. How did astropy do it?
> > > Since astropy are dealing with these types of dependencies, I think investigating how they do that and copying their solution is the simplest path forward.
> > 
> > 
> > Yes, they just checked `isInstance(t.show_in_table(), Datagrid)` but again this wouldn't work here, because we are not returning Datagrid as they are.
> 
> Hmm, I expect that properly testing this kind of output is painful and why it was not done. Can you check how the `quicklook` method is tested inside sunpy?

Yes I had a look, I will try using mocking this will take some time though.

Solves #6795 
Related To #6814 

```
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

import astropy.units as u

import sunpy.data.sample
import sunpy.map
map_aia = sunpy.map.Map(sunpy.data.sample.AIA_171_IMAGE)
map_hmi = sunpy.map.Map(sunpy.data.sample.HMI_LOS_IMAGE)
map_hmi.plot_settings['cmap'] = "hmimag"
map_hmi.plot_settings['norm'] = plt.Normalize(-1500, 1500)
```

```
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(12, 5))
ax1 = fig.add_subplot(121, projection=map_aia)
map_aia.plot(axes=ax1, clip_interval=(1, 99.9)*u.percent)
ax2 = fig.add_subplot(122, projection=map_aia)
map_hmi.plot(axes=ax2, autoalign=True, title='HMI image in AIA reference frame')
ax2.axis(ax1.axis())
```

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/354b2f5c-d01b-4021-ad00-0edad8422736)

Now with `autoalign='reproject'`

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/336e2ab2-8920-4349-bb1a-2d9b5d6323e0)

Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

To address this, I created a function that handles this logic. It selectively updates `contour_args` with relevant entries from `plot_settings` while removing unsupported or conflicting keys. This allows the user to override arguments like `origin` without having them unintentionally set.

Here’s the detailed reasoning:

- **`cmap`:** Setting `cmap = None` avoids the error:  
  > *ValueError: Either colors or cmap must be None*  
  Since contour plots handle colors separately via the `colors` argument, passing a colormap (`cmap`) is unnecessary.

- **`interpolation`:** Matplotlib's `contour` does not use this argument. Passing it results in the warning:  
  > *The following kwargs were not used by contour: 'interpolation'*  
  Therefore, this argument is removed.

- **`origin`:** If `'origin': 'lower'` is specified, it is updated to `'origin': None` because `None` is the default for contour plots in Matplotlib. This ensures that the behavior aligns with Matplotlib's expected defaults.


- **`norm`:** The condition:
  A better approach is to let the user control the norm setting in plot_settings['norm'] for this the below logic is to be implemented but its not quite working. 
  ```python
    if contour_args['norm'] == self.norm_to_check_contour:
        contour_args['norm'] = None

","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/2482870722/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/pulls/comments/1847568297","pull_request_review_id":2444179940,"id":1847568297,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOACEKh85uH6ep","diff_hunk":"@@ -2445,6 +2447,64 @@ def _process_levels_arg(self, levels):
             raise u.UnitsError("This map has no unit, so levels can only be specified in percent "
                                "or in u.dimensionless_unscaled units.")
 
+
+    def _update_contour_args(self, contour_args):
+        """
+        Updates `contour_args` with values from `plot_settings`, ensuring
+        compatibility with Matplotlib's contour plotting. Removes unsupported
+        or unnecessary keys and modifies values for consistency with contour
+        defaults.
+
+        This method modifies the input dictionary `contour_args` to reflect the
+        appropriate settings for contour plotting, avoiding issues with non-compatible
+        keywords like 'cmap' or 'interpolation'.
+
+        Parameters
+        ----------
+        contour_args : dict
+            A dictionary of arguments to be used for contour plotting.
+
+        Returns
+        -------
+        dict
+            The updated `contour_args` dictionary, with unsupported arguments removed
+            and default values applied where necessary.
+
+        Notes
+        -----
+        - 'cmap': Set to `None` to avoid the error "ValueError: Either colors or cmap must be None".
+        - 'interpolation': Removed because Matplotlib's contour function raises the warning
+        "The following kwargs were not used by contour: 'interpolation'".
+        - 'origin': If `'origin': 'lower'` is present, it is replaced with `'origin': None`,
+        as `None` is the default value for Matplotlib's contour plots.
+        """
+        plot_settings = self.plot_settings.copy()
+        contour_args.update(plot_settings)
+
+        # Define default settings for normal plots and contour-specific updates
+        original_plot_defaults = {
+            'origin': 'lower',
+        }
+        default_contour_param = {
+            'origin': None,
+        }
+
+        # Replace conflicting settings with contour defaults
+        for key in original_plot_defaults:
+            if key in contour_args and contour_args[key] == original_plot_defaults[key]:
+                contour_args[key] = default_contour_param[key]
+
+        # if contour_args['norm'] == self.norm_to_check_contour:
+        #     contour_args['norm'] = None
+
+        # Remove unsupported parameters for contour plots
+        contour_args['cmap'] = None  # Contour plots can not use 'cmap' if 'colors' is not None
+        contour_args.pop('interpolation')
+        contour_args['norm'] = None
+
+        return contour_args","path":"sunpy/map/mapbase.py","commit_id":"afa3b3b78773b416bbf80a8c58e0f93103d452c3","original_commit_id":"afa3b3b78773b416bbf80a8c58e0f93103d452c3","user":{"login":"Prtm2110","id":139000226,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEj5og","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139000226?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110","html_url":"https://github.com/Prtm2110","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"I created it to keep things clean and manageable, it's not essential, should I move it inside `draw contour`?

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

 default_contour_param = {
            'origin': None,
        }

  # Replace conflicting settings with contour defaults
  for key in original_plot_defaults:
      if key in contour_args and contour_args[key] == original_plot_defaults[key]:
          contour_args[key] = default_contour_param[key]
```
If the dict pair from (just newly concatenated contour_args) is also present in `original_plot_defaults`(this stores the plot_settings that have been set in the init method of `GenericMap` for map plotting) then this value of the pair is to be replaced with the corresponding key's value from `default_contour_param`(this stores the overlapping matplotlib contour parameter and there default vales)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

dictionary","user":{"login":"Prtm2110","id":139000226,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEj5og","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139000226?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110","html_url":"https://github.com/Prtm2110","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"## PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

+    def _update_contour_args(self, contour_args):
+        """
+        Updates `contour_args` with values from `plot_settings`, ensuring
+        compatibility with Matplotlib's contour plotting. Removes unsupported
+        or unnecessary keys and modifies values for consistency with contour
+        defaults.
+
+        This method modifies the input dictionary `contour_args` to reflect the
+        appropriate settings for contour plotting, avoiding issues with non-compatible
+        keywords like 'cmap' or 'interpolation'.
+
+        Parameters
+        ----------
+        contour_args : dict
+            A dictionary of arguments to be used for contour plotting.
+
+        Returns
+        -------
+        dict
+            The updated `contour_args` dictionary, with unsupported arguments removed
+            and default values applied where necessary.
+
+        Notes
+        -----
+        - 'cmap': Set to `None` to avoid the error "ValueError: Either colors or cmap must be None".
+        - 'interpolation': Removed because Matplotlib's contour function raises the warning
+        "The following kwargs were not used by contour: 'interpolation'".
+        - 'origin': If `'origin': 'lower'` is present, it is replaced with `'origin': None`,
+        as `None` is the default value for Matplotlib's contour plots.
+        """
+        plot_settings = self.plot_settings.copy()
+        contour_args_copy = contour_args.copy()
+
+        contour_args.update(plot_settings)
+
+        # Define default settings for normal plots and contour-specific updates
+        original_plot_defaults = {
+            'origin': 'lower',
+        }
+        default_contour_param = {
+            'origin': None,
+        }
+
+        # Replace conflicting settings with contour defaults
+        for key in original_plot_defaults:
+            if key in contour_args and contour_args[key] == original_plot_defaults[key]:
+                contour_args[key] = default_contour_param[key]
+
+        contour_args['cmap'] = None  # 'cmap' cannot be used for contour plots when levels are not None,in-case of composite maps.
+        contour_args.update(contour_args_copy)  # If 'draw_contour' is used, this ensures the method arguments are applied.
+
+        contour_args.pop('interpolation')
+        contour_args['norm'] = None
+
+        return ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```

After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

dictionary","user":{"login":"Prtm2110","id":139000226,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEj5og","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139000226?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110","html_url":"https://github.com/Prtm2110","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"## PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

dictionary","user":{"login":"Prtm2110","id":139000226,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEj5og","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139000226?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110","html_url":"https://github.com/Prtm2110","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"## PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

dictionary","user":{"login":"Prtm2110","id":139000226,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEj5og","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139000226?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110","html_url":"https://github.com/Prtm2110","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"## PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

dictionary","user":{"login":"Prtm2110","id":139000226,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEj5og","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139000226?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110","html_url":"https://github.com/Prtm2110","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"## PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

dictionary","user":{"login":"Prtm2110","id":139000226,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEj5og","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139000226?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110","html_url":"https://github.com/Prtm2110","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"## PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

dictionary","user":{"login":"Prtm2110","id":139000226,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEj5og","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139000226?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110","html_url":"https://github.com/Prtm2110","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"## PR Description
Fixes #6382 
Before applying `plot_settings` 
```
comp_map = sunpy.map.Map(aia_smap, hmi_smap, composite=True)
levels = [-1000, -500, -250, 250, 500, 1000] * u.G
comp_map.set_levels(index=1, levels=levels)

fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
Edit: I just realised the image below is not what is used to, this needs to be fixed
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/26901c77-0471-449d-98d1-b5a24d32ac5f)
After adding `plot_settings`
```
settings1 = comp_map.get_plot_settings(1)

settings1['linewidths'] = 4
settings1['alpha'] = 1
settings1['zorder'] = 10
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=comp_map.get_map(0))
comp_map.plot(axes=ax)
plt.show()
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df632cf2-bd6e-4364-9ea6-14ec47f328dd)

This PR introduces the Rectangle class with methods to create a rectangular regions using different input options:

`from_corners`: Allows creation of a rectangle by specifying the bottom-left and top-right coordinates.
`from_bottom_left`: Creates a rectangle using the bottom-left coordinate along with width and height.
`from_center`: Enables rectangle creation from the center coordinate, width, and height, automatically calculating the bottom-left and top-right corners.

Each method can optionally return a tuple of `(bottom_left, top_right)` if `as_tuple=True`, making it flexible to work with both rectangle objects and raw coordinates.

Solves #6522

```
if autoalign == 'reproject':
    if axes.get_xlim() == (0, 1) and axes.get_ylim() == (0, 1):
        # Set plot limits based on the extent of the map
        naxis1 = self.meta.get('naxis1', self.data.shape[1])
        naxis2 = self.meta.get('naxis2', self.data.shape[0])
        
        # Create an extent list using naxis1 and naxis2 
        axes.set_xlim(extent[0], extent[1])
        axes.set_ylim(extent[2], extent[3])
    
    target_wcs = axes.wcs
    xlim, ylim = axes.get_xlim(), axes.get_ylim()
    
    # Calculate array shape based on limits
    array_shape = (int(np.abs(ylim[1] - ylim[0])), int(np.abs(xlim[1] - xlim[0])))
    reprojected_map = self.reproject_to(target_wcs, shape_out=array_shape)
    data = reprojected_map.data
    print(xlim, ylim)
```
However, I'm currently stuck on how to use `naxis1` and `naxis2`. Specifically, I’m unsure how to use these variables to properly set the extent of the axes or calculate the desired output shape for the reprojected map. Any guidance on how to utilize these values effectively would be greatly appreciated.

+class Rectangle:
+    def __init__(self, bottom_left: SkyCoord, top_right: SkyCoord):
+        """
+        Initializes a Rectangle object with bottom-left and top-right coordinates.
+        """
+        if not (hasattr(bottom_left, 'transform_to') and hasattr(bottom_left, 'spherical')):
+            raise TypeError("Invalid input: bottom_left must be of type SkyCoord or BaseCoordinateFrame.")
+
+        if not (hasattr(top_right, 'transform_to') and hasattr(top_right, 'spherical')):
+            raise TypeError("Invalid input: top_right must be of type SkyCoord or BaseCoordinateFrame.")
+
+        self.bottom_left = bottom_left
+        self.top_right = top_right
+
+    @classmethod
+    def from_corners(cls, bottom_left: SkyCoord, top_right: SkyCoord, as_tuple: bool = False):
+        """
+        Create a rectangle from the bottom-left and top-right coordinates.
+        If `as_tuple=True`, return a tuple of (bottom_left, top_right) instead of a Rectangle object.
+        """
+        top_right = top_right.transform_to(bottom_left)
+        if as_tuple:
+            return (bottom_left, top_right)
+        return cls(bottom_left, top_right)
+
+    @classmethod
+    def from_bottom_left(cls, bottom_left: SkyCoord, width: u.deg, height: u.deg, as_tuple: bool = False):
+        """
+        Create a rectangle from the bottom-left corner, width, and height.
+        If `as_tuple=True`, return a tuple of (bottom_left, top_right) instead of a Rectangle object.
+        """
+        # Validate width and height
+        if width < 0 * u.deg:
+            raise ValueError("The specified width cannot be negative.")
+        if width > 360 * u.deg:
+            raise ValueError("The specified width cannot be greater than 360 degrees.")
+        if height < 0 * u.deg:
+            raise ValueError("The specified height cannot be negative.")
+        if bottom_left.spherical.lat + height > 90 * u.deg:
+            raise ValueError("The specified height exceeds the maximum latitude.")
+
+        top_right = SkyCoord(
+            bottom_left.spherical.lon + width,
+            bottom_left.spherical.lat + height,
+            frame=bottom_left.frame
+        )
+
+        if as_tuple:
+            return (bottom_left, top_right)
+        return cls(bottom_left, top_right)
+
+    @classmethod
+    def from_center(cls, center: SkyCoord, width: u.deg, height: u.deg, as_tuple: bool = False):
+        """
+        Create a rectangle from the center coordinate, width, and height.
+        If `as_tuple=True`, return a tuple of (bottom_left, top_right) instead of a Rectangle object.
+
+        Parameters
+        ----------
+        center : `~astropy.coordinates.SkyCoord`
+            The center coordinate of the rectangle.
+        width : `~astropy.units.Quantity`
+            The width of the rectangle.
+        height : `~astropy.units.Quantity`
+            The height of the rectangle.
+        as_tuple : bool, optional
+            If True, return a tuple of coordinates. Otherwise, return a Rectangle object.
+        """

+        if width > 360 * u.deg:

+        if height < 0 * u.deg:
+            raise ValueError("The specified height cannot be negative.")
+        if center.spherical.lat + height / 2 > 90 * u.deg or center.spherical.lat - height / 2 < -90 * u.deg:
+            raise ValueError("The specified height exceeds the maximum latitude.")
+

PR Description

This PR introduces the Rectangle class with methods to create a rectangular regions using different input options:

`from_corners`: Allows creation of a rectangle by specifying the bottom-left and top-right coordinates.
`from_bottom_left`: Creates a rectangle using the bottom-left coordinate along with width and height.
`from_center`: Enables rectangle creation from the center coordinate, width, and height, automatically calculating the bottom-left and top-right corners.

Each method can optionally return a tuple of `(bottom_left, top_right)` if `as_tuple=True`, making it flexible to work with both rectangle objects and raw coordinates.

Solves #6522

","user":{"login":"Prtm2110","id":139000226,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEj5og","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139000226?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110","html_url":"https://github.com/Prtm2110","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"## PR Description

This PR introduces the Rectangle class with methods to create a rectangular regions using different input options:

`from_corners`: Allows creation of a rectangle by specifying the bottom-left and top-right coordinates.
`from_bottom_left`: Creates a rectangle using the bottom-left coordinate along with width and height.
`from_center`: Enables rectangle creation from the center coordinate, width, and height, automatically calculating the bottom-left and top-right corners.

Each method can optionally return a tuple of `(bottom_left, top_right)` if `as_tuple=True`, making it flexible to work with both rectangle objects and raw coordinates.

Solves #6522

`if not np.__version__.startswith("2."):` could be an option, it leads to no output, which is then incorrectly compared against the expected output, resulting in test failures.

```
>>> from astropy.time import Time
>>> t = Time('1958-01-01T00:00:00', format='isot', scale='tai')
>>> t.tai_seconds  # doctest: +SKIP
0.0
>>> t2 = Time('2015-10-25T05:24:08', format='isot', scale='tai')
>>> t2.tai_seconds  # doctest: +SKIP
1824441848.0
>>> t3 = Time(t2.tai_seconds, format='tai_seconds')  # scale is automatically TAI
>>> t3.isot
```
`t.tai_seconds ` and `t2.tai_seconds` outputs `np.float64(1824441848.0)` and  `np.float64(1824441848.0)`

In `sunpy/docs/tutorial/units.rst`
```
>>> length.value  # doctest: +SKIP
10.0
>>> length.to_value() # doctest: +SKIP
10.0
>>> length.to_value(u.km)  # doctest: +SKIP
0.01
```
All the outputs are in float format but numpy2.0 is outputting them in `np.float64` format

+    def get_contours(self, level, method='contourpy', **kwargs):
+        """
+        Returns coordinates of the contours for a given level value.
+
+        For details of the contouring algorithm, see :func:`contourpy.contour_generator` or :func:`contourpy.contour_generator`.
+
+        Parameters
+        ----------
+        level : float, astropy.units.Quantity
+            Value along which to find contours in the array. If the map unit attribute
+            is not `None`, this must be a `~astropy.units.Quantity` with units
+            equivalent to the map data units.
+        method : {'contourpy', 'skimage'}
+            Determines which contouring method is used and should
+            be specified as either 'contourpy' or 'skimage'.
+            Defaults to 'skimage'.
+        kwargs :
+            Additional keyword arguments passed to either :func:`contourpy.contour_generator`
+            or :func:`skimage.measure.find_contours`, depending on the value of the `method` argument.
+
+        Returns
+        -------
+        contours: list of (n,2) `~astropy.coordinates.SkyCoord`
+            Coordinates of each contour.
+
+        Examples
+        --------
+        >>> import astropy.units as u
+        >>> import sunpy.map
+        >>> import sunpy.data.sample  # doctest: +REMOTE_DATA
+        >>> aia = sunpy.map.Map(sunpy.data.sample.AIA_171_IMAGE)  # doctest: +REMOTE_DATA
+        >>> contours = aia.get_contours(50000 * u.DN, method='contourpy')  # doctest: +REMOTE_DATA
+        >>> print(contours[0])  # doctest: +REMOTE_DATA

+    def get_contours(self, level, method='contourpy', **kwargs):
+        """
+        Returns coordinates of the contours for a given level value.
+
+        For details of the contouring algorithm, see :func:`contourpy.contour_generator` or :func:`contourpy.contour_generator`.
+
+        Parameters
+        ----------
+        level : float, astropy.units.Quantity
+            Value along which to find contours in the array. If the map unit attribute
+            is not `None`, this must be a `~astropy.units.Quantity` with units
+            equivalent to the map data units.
+        method : {'contourpy', 'skimage'}
+            Determines which contouring method is used and should
+            be specified as either 'contourpy' or 'skimage'.
+            Defaults to 'contourpy'.
+        kwargs :
+            Additional keyword arguments passed to either :func:`contourpy.contour_generator`
+            or :func:`skimage.measure.find_contours`, depending on the value of the ``method`` argument.
+
+        Returns
+        -------
+        contours: list of (n,2) `~astropy.coordinates.SkyCoord`
+            Coordinates of each contour.
+
+        Examples
+        --------
+        >>> import astropy.units as u
+        >>> import sunpy.map
+        >>> import sunpy.data.sample  # doctest: +REMOTE_DATA
+        >>> aia = sunpy.map.Map(sunpy.data.sample.AIA_171_IMAGE)  # doctest: +REMOTE_DATA
+        >>> contours = aia.get_contours(50000 * u.DN, method='contourpy')  # doctest: +REMOTE_DATA
+        >>> print(contours[0])  # doctest: +    def get_contours(self, level, method='contourpy', **kwargs):
+        """
+        Returns coordinates of the contours for a given level value.
+
+        For details of the contouring algorithm, see :func:`contourpy.contour_generator` or :func:`contourpy.contour_generator`.
+
+        Parameters
+        ----------
+        level : float, astropy.units.Quantity
+            Value along which to find contours in the array. If the map unit attribute
+            is not `None`, this must be a `~astropy.units.Quantity` with units
+            equivalent to the map data units.
+        method : {'contourpy', 'skimage'}
+            Determines which contouring method is used and should
+            be specified as either 'contourpy' or 'skimage'.
+            Defaults to 'contourpy'.
+        kwargs :
+            Additional keyword arguments passed to either :func:`contourpy.contour_generator`
+            or :func:`skimage.measure.find_contours`, depending on the value of the ``method`` argument.
+
+        Returns
+        -------
+        contours: list of (n,2) `~astropy.coordinates.SkyCoord`
+            Coordinates of each contour.
+
+        Examples
+        --------
+        >>> import astropy.units as u
+        >>> import sunpy.map
+        >>> import sunpy.data.sample  # doctest: +REMOTE_DATA
+        >>> aia = sunpy.map.Map(sunpy.data.sample.AIA_171_IMAGE)  # doctest: +REMOTE_DATA
+        >>> contours = aia.get_contours(50000 * u.DN, method='contourpy')  # doctest: +REMOTE_DATA
+        >>> print(contours[0])  # doctest: +    def get_contours(self, level, method='contourpy', **kwargs):
+        """
+        Returns coordinates of the contours for a given level value.
+
+        For details of the contouring algorithm, see :func:`contourpy.contour_generator` or :func:`contourpy.contour_generator`.
+
+        Parameters
+        ----------
+        level : float, astropy.units.Quantity
+            Value along which to find contours in the array. If the map unit attribute
+            is not `None`, this must be a `~astropy.units.Quantity` with units
+            equivalent to the map data units.
+        method : {'contourpy', 'skimage'}
+            Determines which contouring method is used and should
+            be specified as either 'contourpy' or 'skimage'.
+            Defaults to 'contourpy'.
+        kwargs :
+            Additional keyword arguments passed to either :func:`contourpy.contour_generator`
+            or :func:`skimage.measure.find_contours`, depending on the value of the ``method`` argument.
+
+        Returns
+        -------
+        contours: list of (n,2) `~astropy.coordinates.SkyCoord`
+            Coordinates of each contour.
+
+        Examples
+        --------
+        >>> import astropy.units as u
+        >>> import sunpy.map
+        >>> import sunpy.data.sample  # doctest: +REMOTE_DATA
+        >>> aia = sunpy.map.Map(sunpy.data.sample.AIA_171_IMAGE)  # doctest: +REMOTE_DATA
+        >>> contours = aia.get_contours(50000 * u.DN, method='contourpy')  # doctest: +REMOTE_DATA
+        >>> print(contours[0])  # doctest: I attempted to implement the following code:
```
if autoalign == 'reproject':
    target_wcs = axes.wcs
    xlim, ylim = axes.get_xlim(), axes.get_ylim()
    # Calculate array shape based on limits
    array_shape = (int(np.abs(ylim[1] - ylim[0])), int(np.abs(xlim[1] - xlim[0])))
    reprojected_map = self.reproject_to(target_wcs, shape_out=array_shape)
    data = reprojected_map.data
```
However, it produces a blank plot and raises the following warning:

`WARNING: SunpyUserWarning: The conversion of these 2D helioprojective coordinates to 3D is all NaNs because off-disk coordinates need an additional assumption to be mapped to calculate distance from the observer. Consider using the context manager SphericalScreen(). [sunpy.coordinates.frames]
`

+    aia171_test_map._data = aia171_test_map.data.astype('float32')

+    return fig","path":"sunpy/map/tests/test_plotting.py","commit_id":"66ff91db52131d07f8afc2e1d389246e14ab506a","original_commit_id":"66ff91db52131d07f8afc2e1d389246e14ab506a","user":{"login":"Prtm2110","id":139000226,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEj5og","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139000226?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110","html_url":"https://github.com/Prtm2110","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"> I think my issue with this figure test is that it's hard to know if it worked or we just overlaid the second map on top.
> 
I followed this [Auto_Aligning](https://docs.sunpy.org/en/stable/generated/gallery/map_transformations/autoalign_aia_hmi.html) and tested this code where I used **autoalign='reproject'** and it gave same result as the one in gallery example 
```
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(12, 5))
ax1 = fig.add_subplot(121, projection=map_aia)
map_aia.plot(axes=ax1, clip_interval=(1, 99.9)*u.percent)
ax2 = fig.add_subplot(122, projection=map_aia)
map_hmi.plot(axes=ax2, autoalign='reproject', title='HMI image in AIA reference frame')
ax2.axis(ax1.axis())
```
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3457b4dd-6f39-4ed6-aaac-813d23d702f0)

> Can we also change the color map and maybe pass in an alpha?

In the test right? Okay working on it

import cProfile

test_code = '''

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

import astropy.units as u
for x in range(25):
    import sunpy.map
    from sunpy.data.sample import AIA_193_IMAGE
    aiamap = sunpy.map.Map(AIA_193_IMAGE)
    contours = aiamap.contour(50000 * u.DN)
'''

if __name__ == "__main__":
    cProfile.run(test_code)
```

> * Should we add a note about the possibility of bad quality images and the possible need to normalize by exposure time?
```
#    Depending on the data quality, some images may appear brighter or darker due to variations in exposure time.
#    You can normalize each image by its exposure time to correct for this.
#    This can be done by dividing the data array of each map by its exposure time stored in the map's metadata.

# Example code to normalize by exposure time:
for m in aia_sequence:
    exposure_time = m.exposure_time
    m.data = m.data / exposure_time.to(u.s).value
```
ref [here](https://gist.github.com/wtbarnes/acabdbb0e6c8ea3f85f90ceba5465989)
> * Should we show how to get the full array from the map sequence? and maybe extract a time series from it?
```
# You can also access the full data array from the map sequence 

# Example code to extract an array:
array_from_mapseq  = [m.data for m in aia_sequence_aligned]
```
 Is what I am suggesting correct? And how can I convert `array_from_mapseq` into `Timeseries`

+.. _sunpy-how-to-create-custom-coordinate-objects:
+
+**********************************************
+Create Coordinate Objects with Custom Observer
+**********************************************
+
+It is possible to specify an observer other than Earth by providing a `~astropy.coordinates.SkyCoord` object representing the observer's location.
+This can be useful when working with data from instruments located at specific points in space, such as spacecraft.
+
+.. code-block:: python
+
+    >>> from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord, CartesianRepresentation
+    >>> from sunpy.coordinates import frames
+    >>> import astropy.units as u
+
+Define the observer's position in Heliocentric coordinates
+
+.. code-block:: python
+
+    >>> observer_coord = SkyCoord(-100*u.arcsec, 500*u.arcsec, frame=frames.Helioprojective)
+    >>> observer_coord= SkyCoord(x=-72241.0*u.km, y=361206.1*u.km, z=589951.4*u.km, frame=frames.Heliocentric)
+    >>> observer_coord = SkyCoord(70*u.deg, -30*u.deg, frame=frames.HeliographicStonyhurst)
+    >>> observer_coord
+    <SkyCoord (HeliographicStonyhurst: obstime=None, rsun=695700.0 km): (lon, lat) in deg
+        (70., Gallery"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-06-15T16:23:58Z","updated_at":"2024-07-16T06:54:42Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Provide a general description of the issue or problem.

A common task when dealing with AIA data (or any imaging data in time) is to track a particular region as a function of time and then create a cutout around that region and align it at each time step to get an aligned datacube. There are many ways of performing that alignment, but one  of the most common is to remove the effect of solar rotation.

These steps can easily be done using `reproject_to` and `propagate_with_solar_surface` as demonstrated in [this slide deck from my talk at SciPy in 2023](https://wtbarnes.github.io/scipy-2023-talk/#/inspecting-data-with-sunpy). We should have a gallery example that shows how to do this on a small set of AIA images spaced far enough in time that the effect of solar rotation is obvious (eg spaced out over a day at 6 h intervals). Essentially, this just boils down to choosing a WCS that represents the cutout you're interested in and reprojecting all slices to that cutout,

```python
with propagate_with_solar_surface():
  m_seq_aligned = sunpy.map.Map([m.reproject_to(m_cutout.wcs) for m in m_seq], sequence=True)

```
import sunpy.map
from sunpy.net import Fido, attrs as a
from sunpy.coordinates import frames

from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord
import astropy.units as u
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.image as mpimg

timerange = a.Time('2023-07-01T00:00:00', '2023-07-01T18:00:00')
instrument = a.Instrument('AIA')
wavelength = a.Wavelength(171 * u.angstrom)
result = Fido.search(timerange & instrument & wavelength)

downloaded_files = Fido.fetch(result)

maps = sunpy.map.Map(downloaded_files, sequence=True)

cutout_center = SkyCoord(-500 * u.arcsec, 200 * u.arcsec, frame=frames.Helioprojective, obstime=maps[0].date)
width = 300 * u.arcsec
height = 300 * u.arcsec

cutout_frame = sunpy.map.make_fitswcs_header(
    (height, width),
    cutout_center,
    maps[0].coordinate_frame)

m_cutout = sunpy.map.Map(maps[0].data, cutout_frame)

with propagate_with_solar_surface():
    aligned_maps = sunpy.map.Map([m.reproject_to(m_cutout.wcs) for m in maps], sequence=True)

for i, amap in enumerate(aligned_maps):
    fig = plt.figure()
    ax = fig.add_subplot(projection=amap)
    amap.plot(axes=ax, annotate=False)
    plt.title(f'Time: {amap.date}')
    plt.axis('off')
    jpeg_filename = f'aligned_image_{i}.jpg'
    plt.savefig(jpeg_filename, format='jpeg', bbox_inches='tight', pad_inches=0)
    plt.close()


```

Provide a general description of the issue or problem.

A common task when dealing with AIA data (or any imaging data in time) is to track a particular region as a function of time and then create a cutout around that region and align it at each time step to get an aligned datacube. There are many ways of performing that alignment, but one  of the most common is to remove the effect of solar rotation.

These steps can easily be done using `reproject_to` and `propagate_with_solar_surface` as demonstrated in [this slide deck from my talk at SciPy in 2023](https://wtbarnes.github.io/scipy-2023-talk/#/inspecting-data-with-sunpy). We should have a gallery example that shows how to do this on a small set of AIA images spaced far enough in time that the effect of solar rotation is obvious (eg spaced out over a day at 6 h intervals). Essentially, this just boils down to choosing a WCS that represents the cutout you're interested in and reprojecting all slices to that cutout,

```python
with propagate_with_solar_surface():
  m_seq_aligned = sunpy.map.Map([m.reproject_to(m_cutout.wcs) for m in m_seq], sequence=True)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7676/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7676/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/2231109243","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7676#issuecomment-2231109243","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7676","id":2231109243,"node_id":"IC_kwDOACEKh86E_AZ7","user":{"login":"Prtm2110","id":139000226,"node_id":"U_kgDOCEj5og","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/139000226?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110","html_url":"https://github.com/Prtm2110","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Prtm2110/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-07-16T14:47:00Z","updated_at":"2024-07-16T14:47:00Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Particular region in images as a function of time and then create a cutout around that region and align it at each time step to get an aligned data cubes
